name: Very Berry Cheese Blintzes
recipeIngredient: Mixed berries or raspberries (fresh or frozen, optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the crepe batter: In a blender or food processor, combine egg whites, skim milk, granulated sugar, all-purpose flour, salt (if using), wheat germ, and melted margarine. Blend until smooth. Let the batter stand for 10 minutes to thicken slightly.
2. Cook the crepes: Heat a 7- to 8-inch nonstick skillet over medium-high heat and lightly grease it. Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the hot skillet and tilt the pan to spread the batter evenly on the bottom. Cook until the top looks dry, about 45 seconds. Turn the crepe and cook for another 20 seconds.
3. Stack the cooked crepes between sheets of waxed paper. Crepes can be wrapped tightly and frozen up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator.
4. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
5. Prepare the filling: In a medium bowl, combine ricotta cheese, cream cheese, sour cream, wheat germ, sugar, and vanilla extract. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of filling onto the center of each crepe. Fold the sides of the crepe over the filling, then fold up the ends to form a rectangle.
6. Place the blintzes in the prepared baking dish and brush lightly with melted margarine. Bake for 10-15 minutes, or until heated through.
7. To serve, top each blintz with fresh berries and additional sour cream. Sprinkle with wheat germ.
NOTE: Blintzes may be covered tightly and refrigerated several hours or overnight before baking. When ready to bake, uncover and proceed with the recipe as directed.
Per serving (1 blintz): Approximately 140 calories, 10g protein, 15g carbohydrates, 4g fat, 26% of calories from fat, 0mg cholesterol, 160mg sodium.
